description:
Securely validate webhooks, API signatures, and incoming secret tokens right inside your Bubble workflows. Secret Validator provides a lightweight, server-side action designed to perform cryptographic validations (such as HMAC signatures or token verification) without exposing your sensitive keys or private credentials to the client. Keep your external integrations tight, secure, and fully verified.

Key Features:
🔒 Secure Server-Side Validation: Processes your verification tokens exclusively on the server to prevent data leaks.

⚡ Ultra-Fast Execution: Uses highly optimized logic with zero unnecessary dependencies for lightning-fast workflow speeds.

🤝 Seamless Integration: Perfect for verifying webhooks from third-party platforms (like Stripe, GitHub, Shopify, or custom APIs) before processing data.
name: Secret Validator
plugin_instructions:
[b][size=5]Setup Instructions[/size][/b]

[b]Secret Validator[/b] allows you to securely verify incoming webhooks or API request signatures entirely on the server-side using a shared secret key.

[b][size=4]1. Add Your Secret Key[/size][/b]
Before using the action, you must provide your private endpoint secret (provided by Stripe, GitHub, Shopify, etc.):
[list]
[*]Go to the [b]Plugins[/b] tab in your Bubble app settings.
[*]Locate [b]Secret Validator[/b].
[*]Enter your private key into the designated [b]Secret[/b] input field (make sure to set it for both development and production versions if applicable). [i]This keeps your key safely hidden on the server.[/i]
[/list]

[b][size=4]2. Add the Action to your Workflow[/size][/b]
In your backend workflows (usually an API Workflow built to handle incoming webhooks), add the [b]Secret Validator[/b] server-side action as the very first step.

[b][size=4]3. Configure the Workflow Fields[/size][/b]
The action requires the following parameters:
[list]
[*][b]Payload / Body:[/b] The raw string or text body received from the webhook provider. 
[*][b]Signature / Token:[/b] The signature header sent by the provider (e.g., X-Hub-Signature or Stripe-Signature) that you need to validate.
[/list]

[b][size=4]4. Handle the Result[/size][/b]
The action will output a boolean value. 
[list]
[*]Add a [b]Conditional Decision (Only when...)[/b] to your subsequent workflow steps.
[*]Only allow your workflow to process data if the result of the validation step [b]is "yes"[/b]. 
[*]If the result is [b]"no"[/b], terminate the workflow or return a 401 Unauthorized response to block malicious requests.
[/list]
